items.title: Has anyone tried Karpathy's Ralph loop on a 7B model?
items.author: u/example
items.summary: I tried running a Ralph-loop critique on a 7B local model — model evaluates its own output, identifies weakest claims, then revises. Got noticeable gains on long-form reasoning but quality degraded after iteration 3. Curious whether the LLM Council pattern stabilises it.

items.title: Minimal Prompts Recover Most of the Gain
items.author: Doe et al.
items.summary: We show that a deliberately minimal prompt template recovers most of the performance gain attributed to elaborate scaffolding on 4 of 6 reasoning benchmarks. The result suggests prompt complexity is often a confound.

items.title: Vibe coding considered harmful?
items.author: thoughtful
items.summary: A short essay arguing that vibe coding accelerates wrong-shape software by suppressing the friction that used to be load-bearing. Counter-arguments invited.
